briefdescription.para: Symbol indicating whether the Low Frequency Clock calibration is present.
Symbol indicating whether the Low Frequency Clock calibration timer is present.
Symbol indicating whether the 192 MHz clock is present.
Symbol indicating whether the Audio clock is present.
Symbol indicating whether the 24 MHz clock is present.
Symbol indicating whether the HFCLK frequency configuration is present.
Symbol indicating whether the ALWAYSRUN register is present for LFCLK.
Symbol indicating whether the ALWAYSRUN register is present for HFCLK.
Symbol indicating whether the HFCLK clock is present.
Symbol indicating whether the HFCLKSRC register is present.
Symbol indicating whether the PLL section is present.
Symbol indicating whether XO registers are present.
Symbol indicating whether XTAL clock source is present.
Symbol indicating whether XOTUNE registers are present.
Symbol indicating whether the LFCLK clock is present.
Symbol indicating whether the CLOCK type contains LFCLK subtype.
Symbol indicating whether LFCLK has LFXO source.
Symbol indicating whether INTPEND register is present.
Symbol indicating whether the CLOCK type contains LFCLKCTRL subtype.
Symbol indicating whether the CLOCK type contains LFCLKSTAT subtype with SRC and RUN registers.
Symbol indicating whether the CLOCK type contains CONFIG subtype.
Symbol indicating whether LFCLK source change event is present.
Symbol indicating whether SRCCOPY register for LFCLK is present.
Symbol indicating whether clock domain always run setting is available.
Symbol indicating whether high frequency clock domain is present.
Symbol indicating whether high frequency clock divide register is present.

detaileddescription.para: Task LFCLKSTART/HFCLKSTART has not been triggered definiton.
Interrupt on LFCLKSTARTED event mask definition.
LF clock SRCCOPY status mask definition.
LF clock SRCCOPY status position definition.
LF clock RUN status mask definition.
LF clock RUN status position definition.
LF clock STAT source mask definition.
LF clock STAT source position definition.
LF clock STAT state mask definition.
LF clock STAT state position definition.
LF clock STAT alwaysrunning position definition.
LF clock STAT alwaysrunning mask definition.
HF clock bit mask in interrupt register.
HF clock RUN status mask definition.
HF clock RUN status position definition.
HF clock STAT state mask definition.
HF clock STAT state position definition.
Internal 32 kHz low power RC oscillator.
Internal 32 kHz synthesized from HFCLK system clock.
Internal 32 kHz low power RC oscillator and external 32 kHz crystal.
Internal 32 kHz RC oscillator and external 32 kHz crystal.
Internal 32 kHz synthesized and external 32 kHz crystal.
External 32 kHz low swing signal. Used only with the LFCLKSRC register. For the others NRF_CLOCK_LFCLK_XTAL is returned for this setting.
External 32 kHz full swing signal. Used only with the LFCLKSRC register. For the others NRF_CLOCK_LFCLK_XTAL is returned for this setting.

briefdescription.para: Low-frequency clock sources.

detaileddescription.para: Used by LFCLKSRC, LFCLKSTAT, and LFCLKSRCCOPY registers.

briefdescription.para: High-frequency clock sources.

detaileddescription.para: Used by HFCLKSTAT and HFCLK192MSTAT registers.

briefdescription.para: Clock domains.
High-frequency clock frequency configuration.

detaileddescription.para: Used by HFCLKCTRL and HFCLK192MCTRL registers.
HF clock bit mask in interrupt register. Interrupt on HFCLKSTARTED event.
Interrupt on LFCLKSTARTED event mask definition. Interrupt on LFCLKSTARTED event.
Interrupt on CTSTOPPED event. Interrupt on HFCLKAUDIOSTARTED event.
HFXO quality issue detected, XOTUNE is needed.
HFXO tuning could not be completed.

briefdescription.para: Interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: Start PLL and keep it running, regardless of the automatic clock requests.
Start calibration of LFCLK RC oscillator.
Stop calibration timer. Start HFCLKAUDIO clock source.

briefdescription.para: Tasks.

detaileddescription.para: The NRF_CLOCK_TASK_LFCLKSTOP task cannot be set when the low-frequency clock is not running. The NRF_CLOCK_TASK_HFCLKSTOP task cannot be set when the high-frequency clock is not running.
Calibration of LFCLK RC oscillator completed.
Calibration timer stopped. HFCLKAUDIO oscillator started.
HFXO quality issue detected, XOTUNE is needed.
HFXO tuning could not be completed.

briefdescription.para: Events.
Function for enabling the specified interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Mask of interrupts to be enabled. Use nrf_clock_int_mask_t values for bit masking.

briefdescription.para: Function for disabling the specified interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Mask of interrupts to be disabled. Use nrf_clock_int_mask_t values for bit masking.

briefdescription.para: Function for checking if the specified interrupts are enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Mask of interrupts to be checked. Use nrf_clock_int_mask_t values for bit masking.

briefdescription.para: Function for retrieving the state of pending interrupts.

simplesect.para: States of pending interrupt are saved as a bitmask. One set at particular position means that interrupt for event is pending.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

simplesect.para: Bitmask with information about pending interrupts. Use nrf_clock_int_mask_t values for bit masking.

briefdescription.para: Function for retrieving the address of the specified task.

detaileddescription.para: This function can be used by the PPI module.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
CLOCK Task.

simplesect.para: Address of the requested task register.

briefdescription.para: Function for setting the specified task.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Task to be activated.

briefdescription.para: Function for retrieving the address of the specified event.

detaileddescription.para: This function can be used by the PPI module.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
CLOCK Event.

simplesect.para: Address of the specified event register.

briefdescription.para: Function for clearing the specified event.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Event to clear.

briefdescription.para: Function for retrieving the state of the specified event.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Event to be checked.
The event has been generated.
The event has not been generated.

briefdescription.para: Function for retrieving the trigger status of the task START for given domain.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Clock domain.
The task START for the given domain has not been triggered.
The task START for the given domain has been triggered.

inbodydescription.para: < LF clock RUN status mask definition.
< LF clock RUN status position definition.
< HF clock RUN status mask definition.
< HF clock RUN status position definition.

briefdescription.para: Function for retrieving the state of the clock.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Clock domain.

Pointer to clock source that is running. Set to NULL if not needed. Ignored for HFCLKAUDIO domain. Variable pointed by p_clk_src must be of either nrf_clock_lfclk_t type for LFCLK or nrf_clock_hfclk_t type for HFCLK and HFCLK192M.
The clock is not running.
The clock is running.

inbodydescription.para: < HF clock STAT state mask definition.

briefdescription.para: Function for changing the low-frequency clock source.

detaileddescription.para: Check in Product Specification if this function can be called when the low-frequency clock is running.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
New low-frequency clock source.

briefdescription.para: Function for retrieving the selected source for the low-frequency clock.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
The internal 32 kHz RC oscillator is the selected source for the low-frequency clock.
An external 32 kHz crystal oscillator is the selected source for the low-frequency clock.
The internal 32 kHz synthesized from the HFCLK is the selected source for the low-frequency clock.

briefdescription.para: Function for retrieving the clock source for the LFCLK clock when the task LKCLKSTART is triggered.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
The internal 32 kHz RC oscillator is running and generating the LFCLK clock.
An external 32 kHz crystal oscillator is running and generating the LFCLK clock.
The internal 32 kHz synthesized from the HFCLK is running and generating the LFCLK clock.

inbodydescription.para: < LF clock SRCCOPY status mask definition.
< LF clock SRCCOPY status position definition.

briefdescription.para: Function for changing the high-frequency clock source.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
New high-frequency clock source.

briefdescription.para: Function for retrieving the selected source of the high-frequency clock.

detaileddescription.para: For SoCs not featuring the HFCLKSRC register, this is always also the active source of the high-frequency clock.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
The internal RC oscillator is the selected source of the high-frequency clock.
An external crystal oscillator is the selected source of the high-frequency clock.

briefdescription.para: Function for changing the Audio clock FREQ_VALUE.

detaileddescription.para: The frequency of HFCLKAUDIO ranges from 10.666 MHz to 13.333 MHz in 40.7 Hz steps. To calculate freq_value corresponding to the chosen frequency, use the following equation: FREQ_VALUE = 2^16 * ((12 * f_out / 32M) - 4)

simplesect.para: Chosen frequency must fit in 11.176 MHz - 11.402 MHz or 12.165 MHz - 12.411 MHz frequency bands.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
New FREQ_VALUE

briefdescription.para: Function for retrieving the Audio clock FREQ_VALUE.

detaileddescription.para:
p_reg
Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
The frequency of HFCLKAUDIO ranges from 10.666 MHz to 13.333 MHz in 40.7 Hz steps. To calculate frequency corresponding to the returned FREQ_VALUE, use the following equation: f_out = 32M * (4 + FREQ_VALUE * 2^(-16))/12

briefdescription.para: Function for changing the calibration timer interval.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
New calibration timer interval in 0.25 s resolution (range: 0.25 seconds to 31.75 seconds).

briefdescription.para: Function for changing the HFCLK frequency divider.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
New HFCLK divider.

briefdescription.para: Function for retrieving the HFCLK frequency divider.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

briefdescription.para: Function for changing the HFCLK192M frequency divider.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
New HFCLK192M divider.

briefdescription.para: Function for retrieving the HFCLK192M frequency divider.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

briefdescription.para: Function for changing the HFCLK192M source.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
New HFCLK192M source.

briefdescription.para: Function for retrieving the selected source of the HFCLK192M.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
The internal RC oscillator is the selected source of the HFCLK192M.
An external crystal oscillator is the selected source of the HFCLK192M.

briefdescription.para: Function for setting the clock domain to always run.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Clock domain.
Ensure the clock is always running.

briefdescription.para: Function for checking if the clock domain is configured to always run.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Clock domain.
The clock domain is configured to always run.
The clock domain is not configured to always run.

briefdescription.para: Function for checking if the clock domain always run setting is active.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Clock domain.
The clock domain always run setting is active.
The clock domain always run setting is not active.

inbodydescription.para: < LF clock STAT alwaysrunning position definition.
< LF clock STAT alwaysrunning mask definition.

briefdescription.para: Function for setting the subscribe configuration for a given CLOCK task.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Task for which to set the configuration.
Channel through which to subscribe events.

briefdescription.para: Function for clearing the subscribe configuration for a given CLOCK task.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Task for which to clear the configuration.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the subscribe configuration for a given CLOCK task.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Task for which to read the configuration.

briefdescription.para: Function for setting the publish configuration for a given CLOCK event.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Event for which to set the configuration.
Channel through which to publish the event.

briefdescription.para: Function for clearing the publish configuration for a given CLOCK event.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Event for which to clear the configuration.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the publish configuration for a given CLOCK event.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Event for which to read the configuration.

briefdescription.para: Function controls reset to the LF CLOCK peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Enable reset.

briefdescription.para: Function for enabling bypass.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Bypass source.
Enable bypass.

briefdescription.para: Function for setting the timeout value for the clock alignment procedure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Number of 32Ki clocks.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the timeout value for the clock alignment procedure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
of 32Ki clocks.

memberdef.argsstring: (NRF_CLOCK_Type *p_reg, uint8_t thr_low, uint8_t thr_high, uint8_t thr_diff)
memberdef.name: nrf_clock_config_threshold_set

briefdescription.para: Function for setting threshold limit while clock switching.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Threshold low limit while clock switching.
Threshold high limit while clock switching.
Threshold difference while clock switching.
